g.data-narration: Welcome to understanding Distributed Consensus - the foundation that makes blockchain technology possible.
Consensus is the process where multiple nodes agree on a single data value or state, even when some nodes fail or behave maliciously.
g.text: Consensus in distributed systems is the process by which multiple nodes agree
on a single data value or state, even when some nodes may fail or behave
maliciously. In blockchain, it ensures all participants agree on transaction validity.
Three nodes reaching agreement through consensus

g.data-narration: In a decentralized network with no central authority, multiple nodes maintain copies of data and must agree on the same state.
g.text: In a decentralized network with no central authority:

g.data-narration: The core problem: nodes must agree on valid transactions, their order, and the current state of accounts without a central authority.
g.text: Network of computers must agree on:
Without consensus, each node has different view → system useless

g.data-narration: Consensus has four essential properties: Agreement, Validity, Termination, and Integrity. Let's explore each one.
Agreement: All non-faulty nodes must decide on the same value. If node A decides block B is valid, node C must also decide the same.
g.text: All non-faulty nodes must agree on the same value

g.data-narration: Validity ensures output reflects proposed values. Termination guarantees all nodes eventually decide, avoiding infinite loops.
g.text: If all propose value V → output must be V

g.data-narration: Integrity: A node can only decide on a value that was actually proposed by some node - no magical values appear from nowhere.
g.text: A node can only decide on a value that was actually proposed by some node

g.data-narration: Crash faults: nodes stop working. Byzantine faults: nodes behave arbitrarily, sending wrong data or lying - the Byzantine Generals Problem.
g.text: Types of Failures Consensus Must Handle

g.data-narration: The CAP Theorem shows blockchains must balance Consistency, Availability, and Partition Tolerance - choosing trade-offs based on needs.
Permissioned blockchains use efficient voting (PBFT) with known participants. Permissionless require Sybil-resistant mechanisms like PoW or PoS.
Traditional databases rely on central authority with ACID consistency. Blockchain uses distributed, trustless consensus with eventual consistency.
Consensus powers cryptocurrency networks, supply chain tracking, healthcare records, voting systems, and IoT device coordination.
To summarize: Consensus enables decentralized agreement, handles Byzantine faults, and provides four key properties - Agreement, Validity, Termination, and Integrity.
g.text: • Proof of Work (PoW) & Proof of Stake (PoS)
Consensus is the foundation for all blockchain functionality
